steps.title: Translate each sentence
steps.content:
Sentence 1: "The sum of a number $x$ and 7 is twice as large as a number $y$" → $x + 7 = 2y$.
Sentence 2: "The number $y$ is 3 less than the number $x$" → $y = x - 3$.

steps.title: Evaluate Each Choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'x+7=2y / y=x−3': First equation matches sentence 1; second matches sentence 2. ✓
  • Choice B — 'x+7=2y / y=3−x': First equation is correct, but second reverses the subtraction making y = 3-x instead of x-3. ✗
  • Choice C — '2(x+7)=y / y=x−3': First equation says y is twice (x+7), meaning y is twice as large as (x+7), but the problem says (x+7) is twice as large as y. ✗
  • Choice D — '2(x+7)=y / y=3−x': Both equations are wrong. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
